Flow is right to left in all photos. (a) Composite of electron micrograph of an array on left, photo of red blood cells flowing into the array on right, and a schematic of a removable RTV cover (blue and green). Note the step in channel length in the middle of the micrograph. (b) Video stills of several cell types in bright field epifluorescence (left) and H33342 stained leukocyte nuclei (right). (c) Blow up of stills in b showing two conformations of neutrophil nuclei. (d) Low magnification photograph of H33342 labeled white blood cells in a variable length array. Each white dot is a leukocyte nucleus with the cell stuck at the entrances to channels as shown in (b) and (c). The channel lengths range from 20 microns at the bottom of the photo to 110 microns at the top of the photo. The spacing of the ``stripes'' of cells is the channel length plus 20 microns for the space between the channels.

Email: rcarlson@molsci.org
